explanation: Missing await: fetchUser returns a Promise, so user is the Promise itself rather than the resolved object. user.name is undefined, and the title renders as "undefined's dashboard" instead of throwing anywhere near the mistake.

Off-by-one: slice's end index is exclusive, so passing entries.length - 1 stops one element early. Asking for the 3 most recent entries returns only 2, and the newest entry never appears in the feed at all.

Wrong operator: this needs &&, not ||. When nickname is undefined the left side is falsy, so || evaluates the right side and reads .length off undefined, throwing a TypeError for exactly the users the check was meant to protect.

Inverted condition: retries are meant for server-side failures (5xx), but this retries everything below 500 instead. A 404 gets retried until the attempt cap while a 503, the one case a retry would actually fix, returns immediately.

Missing None check: dict.get returns None when the key is absent, so a user with no contact record makes this subscript None, raising TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable. The very next line guards name with a default, which shows the author knew the data was sparse.
